title: Salty Custard Dumplings
description: A whole30-friendly, savory custard filling encased in a flaky dumpling wrapper that's perfect for hangover recovery, inspired by traditional Russian dishes.

ingredients.name: 1 cup whole milk
ingredients.notes: Use room temperature for easier whisking.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: For a richer flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: 3 egg yolks
ingredients.notes: At room temperature for best results.

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: Squeezed fresh to infuse extra tang and zestiness.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon sugar
ingredients.notes: Adds sweetness without compromising on flavor.

ingredients.name: Pinch of black pepper
ingredients.notes: For a touch of heat, optional but recommended for some people.

instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together milk, cream, egg yolks, salt, lemon juice, sugar, and pepper. Stir gently until just combined.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat. Add a few drops of water or oil for easy gliding.
Using a pastry brush, lightly grease the dumpling wrappers (ensure they're clean and dry). Spoon about 1-2 tablespoons of custard filling onto each wrapper’s center.
Fold two opposite corners of the wrapper towards the center and pinch to seal. Repeat with remaining ingredients until all fillings are used up.
Gently place the dumplings seam-side down on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone mat to prevent sticking and easy removal.
Bake for 15-20 minutes or until golden brown. Serve immediately as a snack or appetizer.
